title: Ionpath MIBI (Multiplexed Ion Beam Imaging) Protocols -- University of Minnesota TMCs
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"c2vjb","text":"Link to information regarding Ionpath MIBI as used by the University of Minnesota","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Links to company sites: https://www.ionpath.com/ https://www.ionpath.com/mibiscope/

Slides and Reagents from Ionpath for MIBI Slides (Cat#: 567001): specially formulated, high-purity gold-coated slides Reagents MIBI 20X TBS-T (Cat#: 567005) MIBI 10X PBS (Cat#: 567004) MIBI 10X Tris pH8.5 (Cat#: 567003) MIBI diH2O (Cat#: 567002) Current Ionpath company Conjugated Antibody information for human FFPE tissue and mouse FFPE or frozen tissue: https://www.ionpath.com/conjugated-antibodies/ https://www.ionpath.com/antibody-panels/ Ionpath Conjugation kits: https://www.ionpath.com/antibody-conjugation-kit/

Overview of the MIBI staining protocol with Ionpath reagents and slides 1. Prepare and bake the slides 2. Deparaffinization and Rehydration 3. Heat-induced Antigen retrieval 4. Wash with MIBI 20X TBS-T (Cat#: 567005) 5. Blocking 6. Antibody Staining (Metal-conjugated Abs) 7. Fixation and Dehydration 8. Dry and Store the slides if not processing on scope right away.
creator: Mickayla DuFresne-To
